Inversion Atlas

Browse local PCA windows across a chromosome to identify and characterize inversion candidates. Load a precomp JSON in the sidebar (Browse… on the left) to begin scrubbing.

tabs
1 diagnostic · main scrubber: sim_mat, robust |Z|, per-sample lines, K-means PCA, L3 contingency tables
2 candidate focus · deep-dive on a single promoted candidate
3 boundaries · refine each candidate's start/end into approximate boundary zones
4 catalogue · sortable, filterable table of inversions; export TSV / Markdown
5 karyotype · per-candidate sample-level regime breakdown
6 popstats · stack of population-genetic tracks aligned to the chromosome
7 ancestry · per-window ancestry view (K-cluster + delta12 confidence)
8 windows · per-window summary table (|Z|, λ₁/λ₂, SNP counts, L1/L2 membership)
9 confirmed · carousel of candidates marked confirmed on page 2
11 help · vocabulary, hotkeys, and the full feature roadmap

To produce a precomp JSON on the cluster:

Rscript export_precomp_to_json_v3.R --precomp <LGNN.rds> \
    --l1_envelopes ..._d17L1_envelopes.tsv \
    --l2_envelopes ..._d17L2_envelopes.tsv \
    --l2_boundaries ..._d17L2_boundaries.tsv \
    --out LG28.json

local PCA θπ — chromosome-wide diversity scanner
SCHEMA §22 · structural scaffold · panel renderers ship as R-side data lands
The idea
This page mirrors page 1's pipeline (per-window analysis → window×window similarity → MDS → cluster → candidate intervals) but reads per-sample nucleotide diversity (θπ) instead of dosage. Two scrubbers, two independent input signals, same shape of output.
A region that hits BOTH scrubbers' candidate lists is ~certainly real biology — that's the orthogonal validation. A region that hits ONLY this scrubber is something the genotype scrubber can't see (sweeps, balancing selection, Het-class subdivisions, mis-assemblies) — that's where new biology lives.
Required JSON layers (from cluster-side R-pipeline)
layer
R-side script
status
feeds panels

STEP_R39
⚪ not loaded
per-sample lines · θπ-PC inputs

⚪ not loaded

Panel layout (mirrors page 1, top to bottom)
panel
page 1 equivalent
what it shows

#zPanel

#thLinesPanel

per-sample θπ-PC1 lines · K=3 / K=6 coloring
#thPcaPanel

θπ-PC1 vs θπ-PC2 K-means scatter · current window
#thL3Panel
#l3Panel
diversity L3 contingency · per-band θπ subdivision
Scale — locked to win50000.step10000
ANGSD thetaStat emits θπ at four scales (in 02_heterozygosity/03_theta/): win5000.step1000, win10000.step2000, win50000.step10000 (multiscale) and win500000.step500000 (main). The chromosome-wide scrubber locks to win50000.step10000 for three reasons:
scale
windows / chr
sim_mat size
θπ noise floor
verdict
win5000.step1000
~33 000
~4.4 GB
~50–200 sites/win → noise
infeasible in browser
win10000.step2000
~16 500
~1.1 GB
marginal
infeasible in browser
win50000.step10000
~3 300
~44 MB
~500–2 000 sites/win → stable
DEFAULT
win500000.step500000
~66
trivial
very stable
too coarse for breakpoints
Browser memory: sim_mat is n_windows². Page 1 already handles ~5 000 windows ≈ 100 M cells; doubling that crashes most browsers. Below 50 kb, the matrix exceeds 1 GB.
Per-window θπ stability: at 9× coverage with ~10–40 sites/kb of callable density, a 5 kb window has 50–200 callable sites — the per-sample θπ variance is large enough that local-PCA on those values mostly captures noise. At 50 kb you have ~500–2 000 sites per window; the per-sample estimates are stable enough to PCA meaningfully. Below ~30 kb you embed noise, not biology.
Why finer step doesn't buy you breakpoint resolution: page 1's breakpoints are sharp because the local-PCA clustering signal is binary (in/out of inversion). The θπ analog is a diversity gradient across the inversion's flanks, not a step. Even at 1 kb step, the θπ-PCA breakpoint is diffuse over 20–50 kb of genomic space — the underlying biological gradient sets the floor. The finer scales (5/10 kb) are still useful for per-candidate breakpoint inspection (planned, post-v4.0 boundary panel) but not for the chromosome-wide scrubber.
What's shipped now (v3.99 t14e+ continue)
Structural scaffold only — this empty-state, the tab button, layer detection for the three new layers, schema documentation in §22.
Coming in follow-up turns: the six panel renderers (largely reusing page 1's draw functions parameterized by which layer to read), the cross-method candidate-list merge in the catalogue (dosage-only / θπ-only / both / both_overlap_partial), and the per-candidate concordance sub-view (the figure mockups A-F) on the candidate-focus page.
Blocking: the three R-side scripts. STEP_R39 packages STEP_Q05_aggregate_theta.sh's theta.<CHR>.win50000.step10000.tsv.gz (per-sample θπ at 50 kb / 10 kb step from ANGSD pestPG) into the theta_pi_per_window JSON layer; STEP_R40 runs local PCA on those θπ profiles per window with the same neighbourhood as page 1's dosage local PCA; STEP_R41 thresholds the diversity-|Z| into L1 envelopes, re-decomposes inside each, promotes L2s passing thresholds.
Diversity Atlas — landing / scaffold preview
SCHEMA §17 + §23 · layout-only · per-page renderers ship post-v4
The dual-atlas idea
schema-only · phase A
The same chromosome JSON, viewed through two different lenses. The Inversion Atlas (every page you see today) interprets the data to find chromosomal-rearrangement candidates: L1/L2 envelopes, K-means karyotype groups, σ-profile verdicts, breakpoint zones, regime calls. The Diversity Atlas (this preview page) reinterprets the same loaded JSON — without freeing or refetching it — through a population-genetic-diversity lens.
One JSON, two interpretation layers, single button to flip between them. The toggle ships in a follow-up turn (phase B); this page is the structural commitment to what the toggle will reveal.
State slot state.atlas ∈ {inversion, diversity}, persisted to localStorage. Per-page caches (state._l3CacheFp, state.__linesCache) stay populated across mode flips — switching atlases is instant.

Layers marked ⚪ are not loaded from the current JSON. Each layer is consumed by the diversity-mode renderer for one or more pages (right column). Loading any layer activates only the panels it feeds; the rest stay in empty-state.

Diversity dashboard — planned panels
phase C · per-panel work
When the Diversity Atlas mode is fully built, this page becomes the chromosome-level dashboard — a six-panel grid of diversity summaries. Empty-slot mockups below show where each panel will sit. Click any panel header to jump to the corresponding deep-dive page (when rendered).
θπ overview
Genome-wide nucleotide diversity track per chromosome, with high/low diversity regions flagged.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: theta_pi_per_window · related: page 12, page 7
F_ROH summary
Genome-wide F_ROH (inbreeding-from-runs-of-homozygosity) ranked per sample, with cohort distribution.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: per_sample_froh · related: tab 4 (in diversity mode)
ROH atlas
Per-sample ROH segments along the chromosome, stacked sample × position with ROH-density heatmap.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: roh_segments · related: tab 4 (in diversity mode)
Ancestry-Q stratification
Per-window K-cluster Q values stratified by sample group; identifies broodline-specific ancestry blocks.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: ancestry_q · related: page 7, tab 6 (in diversity mode)
Kinship / relatedness
Pairwise kinship matrix heatmap with hatchery-broodline clustering; outliers (cross-broodline) flagged.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: kinship_matrix · related: family overlay (existing)
Family-restricted LD
LD blocks that only appear within specific families; detects family-private inversions or assortment patterns.
░░░ panel mockup ░░░
feeds: family_ld · related: tab 5 (region catalogue)
Phasing roadmap
~3-5 weeks of post-v4 work
phase
name
scope
status
A
schema-only commitment
SCHEMA §17 + §23 documents the dual-atlas concept; this landing page is the visible commitment
✓ shipped (this turn)
B
toggle button + state slot
Add atlas-mode toggle button in the top bar; state.atlas persists; pages re-read on flip
⚪ next turn
C
per-page diversity-mode renderers
One renderer per forked page (boundaries → ROH atlas; karyotype → ancestry-Q strat; etc.). Multi-turn.
⚪ multi-turn

catalogue + bundle forking
Inversion catalogue → region catalogue; manuscript bundles fork by atlas mode; export shapes diverge
⚪ later
Cross-references
• page 12 (local PCA θπ) — already-shipped diversity scrubber; mirrors page 1's pipeline on per-sample θπ instead of dosage. The orthogonal-validation tool that pairs with the inversion-mode page 1.
• page 7 (popstats) — atlas-shared track stack including θπ, FST, Hobs/Hexp, ancestry Δ12. Same panel in both atlases.
• page 8 (ancestry) — atlas-shared per-window K-cluster assignment + delta12 confidence. Source for the ancestry-Q stratification panel above.
• SCHEMA §17 — atlas-mode-switching specification (state slot, page mapping, what stays same vs forks).
• SCHEMA §23 — this page's specification (added in v4).
• Help page (tab 12) — the "Atlas mode switching" entry under the planned-features table summarizes the same vision in narrative form.
▰ Genome Atlas — landing / scaffold preview
SCHEMA §28 · layout-only · per-page renderers ship as cluster-side genome layers land
The third atlas
schema-only · phase A
The Inversion Atlas reads the chromosome JSON to find structural rearrangements; the Diversity Atlas reinterprets the same data through a population-genetic-diversity lens. The Genome Atlas is the third sibling: it grounds both in the actual reference assembly + its annotations.
Where the other two atlases ask "what is segregating in this cohort?", the Genome Atlas asks "what is the genome itself?" — assembly quality, chromosome architecture, gene content, repeat landscape, conserved synteny with related Siluriformes, and the variant annotations that let inversion candidates be interpreted in terms of disrupted genes or regulatory elements.
Three contexts the cohort lives in: this Genome Atlas (the reference) — Inversion Atlas (what's polymorphic in 226 samples) — Diversity Atlas (how diverse / inbred / structured the cohort is). The Genome Atlas is where you cross-reference an inversion to its gene cargo (phase 12) without leaving the tool.
Required data layers
consumed from cluster-side genome-annotation pipeline

source

Chromosome overview (planned panel)
mockup
One row per chromosome, length-scaled. Each row stacks: gene density, repeat density, conserved-element density, current cohort's L2 envelopes, current cohort's promoted candidates. Click a chromosome to drill into it.
LG01
28.4 Mb
LG02
31.2 Mb
…
26 more chromosomes
LG28
19.7 Mb
Highlighted row = current chromosome loaded in the Inversion Atlas. Mockup gradients stand in for real density tracks; all renderers ship as the corresponding layers land.
Planned panels
phase B+
Assembly QC banner
BUSCO single-copy %, contig N50, scaffold N50, gap rate, T2T completeness chip per chromosome.
Chromosome strip
Length-scaled per-chromosome rows with stacked density tracks (gene / repeat / conserved). Click to drill.
Gene cargo overlay
For each promoted candidate in the Inversion Atlas, list the genes inside the inversion span. Highlight deleterious-variant overlap from VESM / SIFT4G / SnpEff.
TE / repeat panel
Per-class repeat density across the chromosome. Test breakpoint zones for repeat enrichment (a known mechanism for inversion fixation).
Synteny ribbon
Pairwise collinearity blocks vs related Siluriformes (e.g. C. macrocephalus, I. punctatus). Ancestral karyotype reconstruction from Cactus.
Conserved-element atlas
UCEs and phastCons-derived conserved elements as a sub-track. Used to score regulatory disruption when a conserved element falls in an inversion zone.
Variant-annotation overlay
SnpEff impact categories (HIGH / MODERATE / LOW / MODIFIER) summarized per inversion candidate.
Phasing roadmap
ship-when-ready
phase A
This scaffold — schema commitment, layer inventory, panel mockups. [shipped — turn 53]
phase B
Chromosome overview renderer — one row per chrom, length-scaled, with gene-density + repeat-density tracks. Wires chromosome_map + gene_track + repeat_track layers.
phase C
Gene-cargo overlay on candidates — for each promoted inversion, list the genes inside its span. Surfaces deleterious-variant overlap when MODULE_CONSERVATION outputs land.
phase D
Synteny + ancestral karyotype — Cactus pairwise blocks rendered as a ribbon track. Cross-references the Inversion Atlas's σ-verdicts at chromosome scale.
phase E
Variant-annotation overlay — SnpEff/VEP impact categories per candidate. Final piece for the "what does this inversion break?" question.
Cross-references
see also
• Inversion Atlas (tabs 1–11) — the candidate-curation tool. Genome Atlas contextualizes its candidates with gene cargo, repeat density at breakpoints, and conserved-element overlap.
• Diversity Atlas (tab 13) — the per-sample diversity / ROH / kinship interpretation of the same JSON. The two non-Inversion atlases share the "render-only, compute external" architecture (turn 53 architectural note).
• Population Atlas (separate HTML) — sibling deliverable covering Samples / QC / Families / Heterozygosity / Diversity / Inbreeding.
• SCHEMA §28 — this page's specification (added in turn 53).
• MODULE_CONSERVATION (cluster-side) — emits the deleterious-variant calls (VESM / SIFT4G / SnpEff / GERP++) that feed the gene-cargo overlay.
• Cactus alignment — feeds synteny_blocks + conserved_elements layers.

Local PCA scrubber on GHSL — phased haplotype similarity stream

Third of the four discovery streams (dosage · θπ · GHSL · Q). Mirrors the page 1 pipeline (per-window analysis → window×window similarity → MDS → cluster → candidate intervals) but driven by Group-level Haplotype Similarity by Locus instead of dosage or θπ.

Drag-drop <chr>_phase2_ghsl.json on this atlas to populate. Layers marked ⚪ activate the panels they feed; the rest stay in empty-state. Click the green schema badge in the header for the full registry of all known layers.
Panel layout (mirrors page 1 / page 12)
When the GHSL layers load, this page will render the same six-panel layout as page 1 and page 12: (top) per-window |Z| waveform with cand / L1 / L2 / win zone bars; (middle) per-sample lines colored by GHSL K-cluster assignment, with PC1 / PC2 toggle; (left) sim_mat heatmap of GHSL window×window similarity (multi-scale: nn40 → nn320, default nn320); (right) K-means scatter on PC1 / PC2 of the GHSL local-PCA loadings; (bottom) L3 contingency tables — same recluster + Hungarian-aligned coloring as page 1.
No candidate selected.
Promote an interval to the candidate list, then open it here. Two paths:
— page 4 catalogue: select an L2 row (or L1-merged group) → 👁 view as candidate, or
— page 1 diagnostic: lock colors on an L2 → ⬆ promote to candidate.
Promoted candidates start provisional; flip mark confirmed here once reviewed.
