Load a precomp JSON to view the per-window summary.
per-window strip · color = |Z| · click a tick to jump · L1/L2 zone bars on top
biallelic SNPs called from genotype likelihoods (low-coverage WGS).
ANGSD biSNP discovery — C. gariepinus 226-sample hatchery cohort
SNP discovery on per-chromosome chunks of fClaHyb_Gar_LG.fa (28 pseudochromosomes, 963.9 Mb). Computed from BAMs filtered through MODULE_1 QC (depth, mapping rate, t-len). Per-chromosome chunked array runs (S06_run_bisnp_discovery_chunks.slurm), output as .mafs.gz for downstream BEAGLE imputation + PCA.
Parameters (MODULE_2A canonical, applied uniformly across all 28 chromosomes):
-GL 1SAMtools genotype-likelihood model — standard for low-coverage WGS
-minQ 25Minimum base quality
-minMapQ 25Minimum mapping quality
-baq 1BAQ recalibration
-C 50Excessive-mismatch downweight
-setMinDepthInd 3Min per-individual depth (from MODULE_1 QC distribution)
-setMaxDepthInd 57Max per-individual depth (from MODULE_1 QC distribution)
-minInd 200Min individuals with data per site (200/226 = 88%)
-SNP_pval 1e-6SNP significance threshold
-minMaf 0.05Minimum minor allele frequency
-pestFolded SFS prior (no outgroup; F₁ hybrid hatchery violates HWE — folded by design)
For other cohorts, the precomp export script (planned, schema 2.15 §8.x) emits these parameters as a top-level angsd_bisnp_params object so this panel can read them generically. This particular tooltip text is hard-coded for the gariepinus project's known canonical run.
filter by L2: min |Z|:
w# center Mb n bi-SNPs span kb bi-SNPs / kb |Z| λ₁ λ₂ λ₁/λ₂ L1 L2
— / — confirmed candidates · ←/→ to navigate · uncheck "mark confirmed" on page 2 to remove
No confirmed candidates yet.
Mark candidates as confirmed using the checkbox on page 2 candidate focus. Confirmed candidates appear here in a carousel — useful for stepping through the final inversion set for manuscript figures.

Marker panels

(no marker layer loaded)
Diagnostic PCR marker panels for each candidate inversion regime. Panels reproduce the genome-based regime call (g0 / g1 / g2 from fish_regime_calls.tsv) using 3–10 markers per candidate. See SCHEMA §10 for column contracts. Activates when marker_panel_summary is in _layers_present.

Boundary zone refinement

Refines promoted candidates into approximate left/right boundary zones. Hotkeys: E override left, F override right, A accept auto, B save, R reset.

Quick reference

Tabs

1 local PCA |z|Main scrubber — sim_mat, robust |Z|, pop-stat tracks, K-means PCA, L3 contingency. Where you do exploratory inversion calling on the dosage signal — tune K, lock colors, draft + commit candidates.
2 local PCA θπSame scrubber, but on per-sample θπ (nucleotide diversity) instead of dosage. Orthogonal validation of inversion candidates + discovery of regions only the diversity signal sees (sweeps, balancing selection). Empty until R-side ships the θπ layers (STEP_R39/R40/R41). SCHEMA §22.
3 candidate focusDeep-dive on a single promoted candidate. Multi-panel grid (sim_mini, karyogram, dosage heatmap, ancestry strip), prev/next navigation. Toggle ⭐ mark confirmed here to populate the confirmed-walkthrough tab. Two-track candidates show per-track regime chips here (turn 48).
4 boundariesRefine each promoted candidate's [start_bp, end_bp] into approximate left/right boundary zones using SV anchors + flanking PC1. Auto-propose + manual override (E/F at cursor). SCHEMA §12. Edits persist across reloads (turn 51).
5 catalogueSortable, filterable catalogue of all L2 envelopes (or L1-merged regions). Hover any column header for the definition. Export selected rows as TSV or Markdown. 📋 export registry ships per-track-aware TSV+JSON for downstream cluster pipeline (turn 45). 📝 manuscript bundle ships an LLM-ready Markdown + companion TSV (turn 50).
6 karyotype / tierTwo views in one page. Karyotype — per-promoted-candidate sample-level regime breakdown. Tier — 14-axis classification view (existence A-D, boundaries, groups, internal structure, mechanism, age, burden, tier). Tier renders empty until the cluster-side final_classification.json layer is loaded via 📦 load registry. Two-track candidates show a 7-column karyotype with per-track membership (turn 43).
7 popstatsStack of population-genetic tracks (Z, SNP density, BEAGLE uncertainty, coverage, θπ, FST, Hobs/Hexp, ancestry Δ12) aligned to the chromosome. Click chips to toggle visibility.
8 ancestryPer-window ancestry view: K-cluster assignment + delta12 confidence heatmap, sample × window. Driven by the ancestry layer in precomp.
9 windowsPer-window summary table — robust |Z|, λ₁/λ₂, eigenvalue ratio, SNP counts, L1/L2 envelope membership. Click any row to jump to that window. Coloring follows whichever sortable column you click (one click sets sort + color).
10 confirmedCarousel walk-through of all candidates marked confirmed on page 3. ←/→ to step through — useful for assembling manuscript figures from the final inversion set.
11 markersDiagnostic PCR marker panels per candidate (3–10 markers tagging each K=3 regime). Hidden until phase-13 marker JSONs are loaded. SCHEMA §10.
12 helpThis page.
13 diversity atlasLanding page / scaffold for the planned Diversity Atlas mode (θπ, F_ROH, ROH atlas, ancestry-Q stratification, kinship/relatedness, family-LD). Layout-only; per-page renderers ship as R-side layers land. SCHEMA §17 + §23.
14 genome atlasLanding page / scaffold for the planned Genome Atlas mode — chromosome-scale assembly stats, gene tracks, repeats / TE landscape, conserved elements (Cactus / phastCons), synteny vs related Siluriformes, variant annotations (SnpEff / VEP). Layout-only; per-page renderers ship as cluster-side genome-annotation layers land. SCHEMA §28.

Vocabulary

L1 / L2 / L3Coarse → fine PCA window scales. L2 envelopes are what the scrubber clusters and what the catalogue rows correspond to. L3 cuts let you split a single L2 into sub-intervals at commit time without re-running the upstream local PCA.
Window resolutionA candidate is committed at either L2 resolution (the default — boundaries snap to L2-envelope edges) or W resolution (per-window precision; boundaries can fall mid-L2). Set on commit; round-trips in JSON. SCHEMA §24.
L3 cutA per-window cut inside a candidate's draft, applied at commit time to split the candidate into N sub-intervals (a SPLIT). Each sub-interval is a sibling of the others, linked via parent_split_id. SCHEMA §24.
IntervalA contiguous span of L2s that you might want to treat as one unit. Drafted in candidate mode on tab 1 (arrow-up extends, arrow-down shrinks; F flip, B set boundary, C cut) or assembled by L1-merging in the catalogue.
CandidateAn interval that has been promoted via ⬆ promote to candidate (tab 1) or 👁 view as candidate (tab 5). Lives on the candidate list in the right sidebar. Starts provisional; flip ⭐ mark confirmed on tab 3 once reviewed. Lane-stacking on the cand-strip resolves overlapping candidates visually. SCHEMA §25.
RegimeWhat each fish (sample) is assigned to within a candidate. For K=3: g0 / g1 / g2 = homo1 / het / homo2 (PC1-rank order). Fish-regime calls export to fish_regime_calls.tsv (per-track since turn 46).
Regime registryUser-curated annotation of "this group of L2s belongs to inversion-system X." Each regime carries a label, axis_topology (SCHEMA §26), L2 members, candidate members (track-scoped since turn 48), and notes. Open via the ⚙ registry button on tab 3's regime row.
Axis topologyRegime-registry vocabulary controlling how a regime aligns to the genome's principal axes: biallelic_inversion, nested_inversion, polymorphism_complex, family_LD, diversity_only, unspecified. User-chosen, never auto-assigned. SCHEMA §26.
TrackA subset of a candidate's K-bands grouped as a single inversion call. Single-track is the default (one track owns all bands). Two-track candidates split bands into two parallel inversion calls sharing the genomic region — common when K≥4 and the σ-profile says TWO_INVERSIONS. Each track has its own active_bands, regime_id, and per-track sample membership. Mutually exclusive: each band belongs to at most one track. (Turns 36-48.)
Atlas trioThree sibling lenses on the same loaded JSON. Inversion Atlas (tabs 1–11) is the curation tool — drafts candidates, manages tracks, refines boundaries. Diversity Atlas (tab 13, SCHEMA §17 + §23) reinterprets the same data through a population-genetic-diversity lens. Genome Atlas (tab 14, SCHEMA §28) grounds both in the reference assembly + annotations. Inversion Atlas computes (you curate); the other two are render-only — they consume layers produced by the cluster-side R/genome pipelines.
Boundary zoneAn approximate ±5-window range around an inferred breakpoint, refined on tab 4. SCHEMA §12 reserves the term exact breakpoint for junction-level evidence (split reads / assembly junctions) — most refinements are zone-level.
σ verdictFor K≥4 candidates, classifies what causes the extra bands. SCHEMA §27 vocab: TWO_INVERSIONS / CROSSOVER_ARTIFACTS / NOISY_REGION / NA / UNDETERMINED. See "σ verdicts" section below for definitions.
Local launcherThe atlas.sh / atlas.bat scripts shipped with the codebase. Start a local HTTP server bound to 127.0.0.1:8765 and open the atlas in your browser. Compared to opening the HTML via file://, the launcher gives you a stable localStorage origin so saved candidates / regime registry / boundary edits persist reliably across sessions. See atlas_launcher_README.md. (Turn 54.)

Key metrics

ConcordFraction of samples assigned to the same K-means group in two adjacent L2s after Hungarian alignment. High = L2s are interchangeable; suggests merge.
Cramér's VEffect-size measure of association between two clusterings. N-independent. <0.10 small, 0.10–0.30 medium, >0.30 large.
p-valueSignificance of the contingency. With persistent sample structure, p ≈ 0 is expected and confirms association — read together with concord.
ConservationMean concord with left + right L2 neighbors. Low (orange) = the L2 boundary is biologically real.
CoherenceCorrelation between two-half mean PC1 vectors within the L2. Low (red, <0.60) = INCOHERENT, suggests over-merge of distinct sub-regions.
Fam purityWithin-cluster fraction from the dominant family. ~1.0 (red, FAM-LD?) = clusters are family-pure → likely family LD, not real inversion. ~0.2 = real biological signal.
SilhouetteCluster separation quality from K-means. >0.5 good, <0.45 weak.
K usedThe number of clusters used for this L2. In adaptive mode, picked per-L2 by silhouette.
σ verdictFor K≥4 L2s, classifies the source of extra bands. SCHEMA §27.

σ verdicts (K ≥ 4 L2s) — SCHEMA §27

TWO_INVERSIONSAll samples have low σ (q50 < 0.05). Joint karyotypes are stable → two stacked inversions.
CROSSOVER_ARTIFACTSBimodal σ distribution with small high-σ tail (2–25% of samples drift). Suggests gene conversion / double crossovers in heterozygotes.
NOISY_REGIONEveryone has high σ. Low informative power.
NAK used ≤ 3. No extra bands to explain.
UNDETERMINEDProfile doesn't cleanly fit any pattern.

K labels TSV (for C++ pop-stats engine)

The export K labels TSV button in the sidebar produces one row per (L2, sample) with columns: chrom, l2_id, l2_idx, l2_start_bp, l2_end_bp, l2_n_windows, ind, cga, family_id, k_used, k_label, k_label_fixed, silhouette, fam_purity, coherence, cluster_ok.

k_label is the adaptive-K assignment (varies per L2 if adaptive mode is on); k_label_fixed is always at state.k (default 3) for cross-L2 comparisons. Group samples by k_label_fixed for uniform pop-stats; by k_label for per-L2 natural clustering.

Pop-stat tracks (--track_<label>)

Pass any per-window scalar TSV at export time:

--track_theta theta.tsv --track_fst_K1K3 fst.tsv --track_pi pi.tsv

TSV must have a value column plus one of:
(a) chrom, start_bp, end_bp, value (range form)
(b) chrom, window_idx, value (1-based index form)
(c) chrom, center_bp, value (center form)

Each track renders as a 60px stacked panel between the Z and PCA panels on page 1.

Color codes (cluster mode)

K-means group → blue / grey / orange / green / red (lo to hi PC1). Samples colored consistently within an L2; Hungarian-aligned to focal in mini-PCAs.

Lock button (🔒 lock colors to current L2) freezes labels from a reference L2 → samples keep those colors as you scrub through other L2s. Useful for tracking how a clean K=3 split holds up across boundaries.

Keyboard shortcuts

← / →Step one window
n / pJump to next / previous L1 or L2 (depending on travel mode)
SpaceToggle play / pause auto-scrub
While drafting a candidate (tab 1, candidate mode):
↑ / ↓Extend / shrink draft right edge
FFlip draft resolution between L2 and W (per-window)
BSet boundary at cursor (split point for SPLIT commit)
CCut draft at cursor — adds an L3 cut at this window
EnterCommit the draft (saves all sub-intervals from cuts as siblings linked by parent_split_id)
EscDiscard draft
While viewing a multi-track candidate (tab 3):
track 1 / 2 buttonsSwitch active track for band selection. Two-track candidates show track-1 and track-2 bands separately in regime row + karyotype.

Note on manuscript writing

Catalogue exports (TSV / Markdown) are designed to drop into Methods / Results sections. They're aids to writing — they don't write the manuscript. Nature acceptance depends on the underlying biology, the prose, and peer review.

Roadmap — what's shipped, what's coming

Tracking what's in flight across recent turns, what's pending in parallel chats, and what still needs designing. Each entry lists what it would take to actually build, so we don't lose the thread between sessions. Faded rows = shipped. Bold rows = active or next-up.

SHIPPED · turns 13–14e (current burst)
Block-presence chips + JSON inspector [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
20 chips per candidate · click to inspect

Page 2 candidate focus now shows a row of 20 chips at the top — one per known structured block file (existence A/B/C/D + boundary L/R + 12 other question blocks = 18 numbered + boundary_right sibling = 19 file types, plus the synthesis final_classification). Each chip:

  • Lit (filled) when that block is loaded for the active candidate; dim (outlined) when missing
  • Green = block contributed at least one axis to the 14-axis classification (per SCHEMA §21.6)
  • Blue = block loaded but supportive-only (no axis derivation: morphology, band_composition, block_detect, triangle_insulation, peel_diagnostic, synteny_dollo)
  • Gold = the synthesis block (final_classification), authoritative when present
  • Grey/dim = not loaded; tooltip explains how to load (drop structured/<block>.json via 📦 load registry on page 4)

Click a lit chip → toggles an inline JSON inspector below the chip row showing the raw block content (header fields like source_script, source_version, computed_at, groups_used.validation_level surfaced separately, then a scrollable <pre> with the full JSON). Click ✕ on the inspector or the chip again to close. Multiple inspectors can be open simultaneously. Blocks larger than 200 KB stringified are truncated with a [truncated] badge — inspect the JSON file directly for the rest.

Block ordering follows SCHEMA §21.3 logical groups: existence (4) → boundaries (2) → carriers (1) → internals (1) → mechanism (1) → age (1) → freq + verdict (2) → morphology + bands (2) → burden (1) → supportive (4) → synthesis (1, always last with ★ icon). Small left-margin between groups for visual sectioning.

What's NOT YET (chat-17+ backlog): per-block renderer panels (rich domain-specific rendering of e.g. internal_dynamics's recombinant table or mechanism's SD evidence) — the JSON inspector shows raw structure today; renderers will be added per-block as schema §21 fields become familiar. Stale-group warnings (chip turns amber when block's groups_used.version doesn't match current sample_groups.tsv created) — also chat-17+. SCHEMA §21.
18 structured block types [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
cluster-side Tier 2 storage · per-block axis derivation

The cluster-side evidence_registry/per_candidate/<cid>/structured/*.json emits 18 distinct block types per Quentin's t14e+ continue paste. Each block has a common header (block_type, candidate_id, region, source_script, source_version, computed_at, groups_used, keys_produced, raw_files) plus block-specific fields.

The 18 blocks at a glance: existence_layer_a/b/c/d (Q1, Q7, NONE), boundary_left/right (Q3, NONE), carrier_reconciliation (Q3/Q6/Q7, IS validation), internal_dynamics (Q2, UNCERTAIN), mechanism (Q4, NONE), age_evidence (Q5, SUPPORTED for FST sub-block), frequency (Q6, SUPPORTED), hypothesis_verdict (Q7, varies), morphology (Q1, supportive), band_composition (Q1, supportive), burden (Q6, VALIDATED), block_detect (supportive), triangle_insulation (supportive), peel_diagnostic (supportive), synteny_dollo (supportive). Plus the optional final_classification synthesis block (alias classification) that is the cluster-side's pre-computed 14-axis result.

What the scrubber does with each block: 12 of the 18 blocks (and the synthesis block) map to one or more 14-axis values via _deriveAxesFromBlock(blockType, obj). Multiple blocks compose: drop any subset of question blocks (e.g. just the 4 existence layers) → Tier view fills 4/14 axes; drop more (mechanism, age_evidence, frequency, etc.) → more axes fill; drop the final synthesis block → all 14 axes overwrite (synthesis is authoritative when present). 6 of the 18 blocks are supportive-only (morphology, band_composition, block_detect, triangle_insulation, peel_diagnostic, synteny_dollo) — they load into state.data.evidence_blocks[cid][block_type] but don't directly map to axes.

Boundary quality reconciliation: boundary_left.json + boundary_right.json are independent files; the scrubber reconciles them at end-of-load. BOTH sides need verdict ~ confirmed_structural AND hardness ≥ 0.7 to qualify as 'sharp'; otherwise 'fuzzy'. The reconciliation runs once per candidate after all files are processed.

Per-block summary on load: the alert dialog now shows known vs unknown block types loaded, count of axis derivations, count of boundary reconciliations, count of synthesis blocks that overrode per-question derivations. Not yet wired (chat-17+ backlog): per-block renderer panels on the candidate focus page, block-presence indicator chips, stale-group warnings (block's groups_used.version vs sample_groups.tsv current created), per-block JSON inspector, manuscript-bundle integration that uses block content for Methods + Results prose. SCHEMA §21.
Registry interop [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
📦 load registry button · multi-file picker

Bulk-load cluster-registry artifacts directly into the scrubber. Quentin's question: "our registry its JSON ?? So I wonder if by any luck we can just load it." Honest answer: partially. The cluster-side registry has four tables (chat-16 design); three are TSV-mirrors of JSON schemas, one is per-candidate JSON blocks. All four load cleanly as optional top-level layers in state.data.

What the 📦 button does: opens a multi-file picker. Drop any subset of these:

  • sample_registry/sample_groups.tsv → state.data.sample_groups
  • interval_registry/candidate_intervals.tsv → state.data.candidate_intervals
  • results_registry/manifest.tsv → state.data.results_manifest
  • evidence_registry/per_candidate/<cid>/structured/*.json → state.data.evidence_blocks[cid][block_type]

Special case: when a structured block of type final_classification (or classification) loads, the 14-axis values are auto-extracted into state.data.final_classification[<cid>] so the Tier sub-view (page 4) populates immediately — drop the registry's classification JSON blocks → see the 14-axis grid populated within seconds.

What does NOT load from the registry: per-window local PCA (sim_mat, |Z|, λ₁/λ₂), dosage chunks, per-window tracks. These live in the precomp JSON outside the registry. Registry-load is additive enrichment on top of a precomp JSON, not a replacement for it.

Validation: light schema-check (required columns/fields, basic types). Full JSON Schema validation is a cluster-side concern. Files that fail validation are rejected with a specific error; the rest of the load continues.

Limitations: .tsv.gz/.json.gz files need pre-extraction (browser can't transparently gunzip without `pako` library, not yet wired). Per-candidate Q matrices and FST scan tracks (results_registry/{candidate,pairwise}/...tsv.gz) are registered in the manifest but data files aren't loaded yet — chat-17+ backlog. SCHEMA §20.
Karyotype / Tier sub-view scaffold [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
14-axis classification empty state		 Page 4 renamed "5 karyotype / tier". New sub-view toggle (Karyotype | Tier ▾) inside the right pane. Karyotype subview keeps its existing per-sample regime table. New Tier subview renders a 14-axis classification grid grouped into six sections (Existence layers A-D, Boundaries, Karyotype Groups, Population Genetics, Biology, Overall Tier). When the cluster-side final_classification JSON layer (planned R-pipeline output: characterize_candidate.R + classify_inversions.R) isn't loaded, the Tier view renders an empty state showing the axis schema with categories per axis but no values. When the layer ships, axis values populate automatically — no scrubber-side change needed. Color palette: green (T1 / VALIDATED / pass / sharp), teal (T2 / SUPPORTED), amber (T3 / UNCERTAIN / fuzzy), red (T4 / SUSPECT / fail), grey (unknown). State slot karyoState.subview ∈ {karyotype, tier}, persisted to localStorage. Read-only contract — scrubber never writes to final_classification. SCHEMA §19.
Windows page coloring + ANGSD bi-SNP info [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
column-driven histogram + provenance chip		 The per-window strip on page 8 now recolors its bars by whichever sortable column the user clicks (one click sets both sort + color, so the histogram and the table tell the same story). New _winSumColorMode state. Modes: z (default), n_snps, span_kb, snp_density, lam1, lam2, ratio, mb, idx. The strip label updates dynamically (was hardcoded "|Z|"). String columns (L1, L2) fall back to z coloring since they're categorical. New ⓘ ANGSD bi-SNPs info chip below the strip — click to expand a panel listing the canonical MODULE_2A parameters used to call the biallelic SNP count for the C. gariepinus 226-sample cohort: -GL 1 -minQ 25 -minMapQ 25 -baq 1 -C 50 -setMinDepthInd 3 -setMaxDepthInd 57 -minInd 200 -SNP_pval 1e-6 -minMaf 0.05 -pest <folded SFS prior>. For other cohorts the precomp export script (planned, schema 2.15) will write these parameters as a top-level angsd_bisnp_params JSON object so the chip reads them generically rather than hard-coding per project.
Candidate bar above L1/L2 [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
pending grey · validated gold ★		 Per Quentin's request: a dedicated candidate strip now sits ABOVE the L1 and L2 zone bars in the Z panel (was previously a thin yellow ribbon below the boundary-arrow band). The new bar shows ALL candidates in state.candidateList, not just confirmed ones, with three visual states:
  • Pending (promoted but confirmed: false) — muted grey fill, no glyph. Visible from the moment of promotion so the user sees their work-in-progress without having to flip to the catalogue.
  • Confirmed (confirmed: true) — shiny gold gradient (pale shine top → mid-gold → deep gold bottom) with a warm gold outline and a ★ glyph centered in bars wider than 12 px. The "validated" state is now visually unmistakable.
  • Draft (active state.l3Draft in candidate-mode) — dashed amber outline only, no fill. Red dashed when has_warning (verdict disagrees with merge).
Render order: faint baseline → all pending (grey) → all confirmed (gold ★) → draft outline. So gold sits on top of grey if they overlap, and the draft outline sits on top of everything. New drawCandidateBar(ctx, d, toX, y0, h, opts) function with opts.dimAlpha for compact-mode dimming and opts.showStar to suppress the glyph. Backward-compat alias drawCandidateStrip forwards to the new function. Layout impact: Z panel reserves candBarH (7 px non-collapsed, 5 px collapsed) + candGap (2 px / 1 px) above the L1/L2 zone bars; plotH shrinks accordingly. 47 new tests (22 regex + 25 runtime) cover state semantics, color stops, glyph suppression on narrow bars, pass order, opts handling.
Refined catalogue export + gallery PNG [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
front-matter + JSON + manuscript-ready gallery

Catalogue export refined:

  • Front-matter metadata — TSV exports get # comment lines, Markdown gets a heading + blockquote. Includes generation timestamp, chromosome, species, schema version + layers, K distribution, σ-verdict distribution, span range, filter + sort context, display + view modes. Reviewer reading the file alone has full provenance.
  • JSON export as third format — round-trippable shape {meta, columns, rows}; meta has the same provenance as TSV/MD; columns carry key/label/type; rows are raw values (no .fmt() truncation). Useful for tools that re-load the catalogue programmatically.
  • Filenames include species slug + ISO date — e.g. C_gar_LG28_Clarias_gariepinus_inversion_catalogue_2026-04-28.tsv. Successive exports no longer overwrite.
  • Helpers: _slugForFilename(), _exportSummaryStats(), _exportFrontMatter(), _exportJsonObject().

Gallery export (📷 gallery: SVG / PNG / PDF, v4 turn 28): one panel per CONFIRMED candidate. SVG is the source of truth (vector, journal-preferred); PNG is rasterized from the SVG via canvas at 4× scale (~300+ DPI for manuscript figures); PDF opens a new window with the SVG and an A4 landscape print stylesheet, then triggers window.print() — user picks "Save as PDF" as destination. Each panel shows display name (turn 24 format, e.g. LG28.15-16Mb.A), chromosomal span (Mb), K, σ-verdict (color-coded for print: green/orange-brown/dark-red/grey/amber), and a band-count bar chart colored by groupColor(). Layout: 4 columns × auto rows. Sorted by start_bp so panels read in chromosome order, top-left to bottom-right. White background for paper printing; system + monospace fonts. Empty-state alert if no confirmed candidates.

Test coverage: 39 regex tests + 32 runtime tests (test_v399_catalogue_export_runtime.js) — verifies _slugForFilename handles edge cases, _exportSummaryStats computes correct K + verdict + span distributions, _galleryVerdictColor returns the print palette, _galleryBandCounts ignores -1 labels, exportGalleryPNG + _buildGalleryCanvas run end-to-end without throwing in a JSDOM-free sandbox.
Species portability [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
first concrete step toward §16		 The scrubber no longer hardcodes "Clarias gariepinus" anywhere user-facing. New optional species top-level JSON field; when present, surfaces in the header bar (italicized scientific name next to the chromosome) and in the manuscript prompt template ("in a {species} cohort"). Fallback when absent: header omits the field; manuscript prompt reads "in the study cohort". Existing JSONs without species continue to work unchanged. Convention: binomial scientific name. R-side export script can populate via a --species CLI flag. SCHEMA §16.6. Direct support for the toolkit-portability vision (§16.1) — one of dozens of small species-agnostic fixes that need to happen. We close the easy ones now.
Lines color-mode picker scaffold [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
SCHEMA §15 UI scaffold for v4.0 multi-evidence renderers		 The mode-picker UI (eight options: kmeans / dosage / ghsl / het / θπ / F_ROH / family / ⚠ confounder) is now sitting in the per-sample lines header bar, sibling to the PC1/PC2 checkboxes. State slot state.linesColorMode persists to localStorage. refreshLinesColorMode() validates the chosen mode against state.layersPresent and falls back to kmeans when the source layer's missing. Greyed-out options carry tooltips naming the missing layer (e.g. "Needs dosage_chunks JSON layer"). Renderer hooks _resolveSampleColorByMode(si, gi, mode) and _resolveSampleScopeColor(si, mode) are STUBS returning null sentinel — callers fall back to existing K-means coloring. New layer per_sample_theta_pi added to detectSchemaAndLayers so the θπ option auto-enables when the future R-side STEP_R36 emit ships its layer. v4.0 work: replace stub bodies with per-mode renderers; UI is already wired so the integration is a one-step swap.
L1/L2 palette unification [done v3.99 t14e+]
ancestry + lines mini + Z zone mode + cand mini		 Four panels still rendered L1/L2 envelope bars in the pre-turn-13 washed-out palette (rgba(79,163,255) + rgba(0,230,118)): the ancestry page (page 7) zone bars, the per-sample lines panel mini L1/L2 strip, the Z panel "zone" color mode for individual |Z| dots, and the candidate-page mini-canvas L1 background tints. All updated to the turn-13 darker palette (rgba(48,116,200) + rgba(34,160,80)); ancestry page also got the "L1" / "L2" y-axis margin labels matching the Z panel. Sim_mat heatmap envelope rectangles deliberately kept in their distinct sim_mat-blue / brighter-green palette (different visual contract — drawn ON a colored heatmap, not next to a plain canvas).
Compact-mode V-bar fix [done v3.99 t14e]
applyMainGrid bails out in compact		 The Cramér's V (anchor concord) bar disappeared in compact mode and only returned after reset-layout. Cause: applyMainGrid() always set inline gridTemplateRows from the fixed-mode panelOrder, beating the compact CSS rule (40px 28px 1.5fr auto 1fr 4px var(--compact-l3fr)). The 28px anchorStripPanel row collapsed. Fix: detect compact mode, clear inline style, return early.
Empty-state with tab guide [done v3.99 t14e]
first-open isn't blank anymore		 Extended #emptyState with tool overview + per-tab one-line description (10 tabs). drawZ renders a centered "Load a precomp JSON in the sidebar (Browse…) to begin" hint when state.data is null instead of leaving the canvas silently blank.
Z panel header strip → hover tooltip [done v3.99 t14d/t14e]
⚙ + ▼ at canvas bottom-left		 The "Robust |Z| · L1/L2 zone bars + boundary peaks · click to jump" strip now lives in #zPanel title= (panel-level hover tooltip). Settings ⚙ + collapse ▼ buttons stack at canvas bottom-left (left:6px / bottom:28px and bottom:6px). #zSettingsBox popover lifted out of the now-hidden parent so it actually opens. pad.t bumped 6 → 14 so L1/L2 bars don't hit the panel top edge.
Minimap hidden when no data [done v3.99 t14d/t14e]
no more empty-square first-open		 setSimInMinimap now holds back the .active class when state.data is null (persistence still works but visual stays hidden). New _reapplyMinimapActiveOnDataLoad() reconciles after JSON load.
📊 Windows jump button [done v3.99 t14d]
left of L2 zoom in viewModeBar		 One-click jump to the windows-page table. Visually distinct from the genome / L1 zoom / L2 zoom buttons (different background) so users don't mistake it for another viewport mode.
PCA right margin fix [done v3.99 t14d]
scatter fills full panel width		 pad.r reduced 200 → 16 in both drawPCA and onPCAClick (must stay in sync for hit-detection). The 200 px gutter was reserved for an external legend; the actual legend is rendered inside the plot at top-right (lx = pad.l + plotW - legendW - 3), so the gutter was empty space.
Lock-click → immediate line recolor [done v3.99 t14d]
drawLinesPanel was missing		 🔒 lock-button click now also calls drawLinesPanel() so per-sample lines recolor instantly. Previously locking bands had no visible effect on the lines panel until the user scrubbed.
Three-axis schema 2.12 + BAM evidence 2.11 [done v3.99 t14a/t14c]
spec-only, no runtime change		 SCHEMA_V2.md 2.10 → 2.12. §12 = BAM evidence layer (9 IGV-style 1 kb tracks, weights pending calibration). §13 = three-axis framework (Tier × Completion × Characterization with 7 questions and 5 statuses). Registry width 45 → 64 cols. Convergence rules in CHARACTERIZATION_CONVERGENCE_RULES.md sibling spec.
Tooltip discoverability sweep [done v3.99 t14b]
column headers, toolbar, tabs, chips		 Every catalogue column has a tooltip field; <th> rendering wires title=. Windows page headers all titled. Top tab bar 11/11 covered. Popstats chips compose tooltip from yLabel + edgeTop/edgeBot. Ancestry chips explain maxQ_label vs delta12. Catalogue toolbar (filter, exports, select-all, count info) all titled.
Confirmed candidates in sim_mat [done v3.99 t13]
green-outlined zones + ID labels		 Confirmed candidates (provisional skipped) render as green boxes rgba(60,192,138,0.85) in the sim_mat heatmap, ID label on top.
Candidate nav strip [done v3.99 t13]
popstats / ancestry / boundaries pages		 Reusable _renderCandidateNavInline() helper. _navigateToCandidate centers cursor + refreshes pages. Whole-genome button clears state.candidate.
Z-panel L1/L2 darker bars + labels [done v3.99 t13]
deeper blue/green		 L1: rgba(48,116,200,...) (was 79,163,255). L2: rgba(34,160,80,...) (was 0,230,118). "L1"/"L2" text labels in y-axis margin.
SHIPPED · v3.99 turns 1–12
Catalogue UX overhaul [v3.99 t10–t12]
favorites, multi-select, simple/detailed		 ⭐ favorites column with persistent storage per-chrom. Ctrl/Cmd+click range select; Shift+click anchor select. simple / detailed column-set toggle. Confirmed-row visual: green left border + ✓ ID chip. Column-flash effect on header hover. Live "n SNPs → n bi-SNPs" relabel.
Z-panel ⚙ settings popover [v3.99 t9]
color/value/highlight controls hidden by default		 Wrapped color-mode + value-mode + highlight-threshold controls in a collapsible #zSettingsBox with a ⚙ toggle. localStorage persistence. Frees Z-panel canvas space; popover restructured in t14e.
Performance: L3 cache + lines pre-render [v3.99 t7–t8]
~120 ms saved per scrub step		 L3 panel cache via _renderL3Fingerprint(curL2). Lines y-matrix cache via state.__linesCache[source]. Offscreen pre-render of untracked-gray cloud blits as one drawImage (~210 polylines × 4302 segments → 1 op). Both auto-invalidate on data load.
L2 zoom mode + viewModeBar [v3.99 t8]
genome / L1 / L2 viewport scopes		 Three-state view selector: 🌍 genome (whole chromosome) / 🔎 L1 zoom (current L1 envelope) / 🔬 L2 zoom (current L2 envelope only). Persisted via localStorage. Z + tracks + lines panels all narrow to the chosen scope.
L3 resize handle 50% height [v3.99 t9]
8 → 4 px slimmer		 Compact-mode L3 resize seam shrunk so it takes less vertical space; hover/drag highlight kept visible.
Karyotype focal-chips fix [v3.99 t6]
K=6 chip rendering bug		 K=6 mode no longer collapses chips to K=3 colors. Focal-chips-above option corrected to compute per-pane palette using the active state.k.
Header / tab UX polish [v3.99 t5]
11px mono unified, no-wrap		 All header buttons standardized to 11 px monospace. viewModeBar moved into the play-row from the header. Active tab gets a vertical alpha gradient. |Z| / ±Z value-mode buttons added. Tracked-samples panel collapse arrow added.
Layout: flex-1 instead of calc(100vh – 76px) [v3.99 t4]
survives zoom changes		 Hardcoded calc didn't survive browser zoom. Now body = flex column, .wrap = flex 1, inner grid uses min-height: 0 for proper shrink. Empty viewport at the bottom gone.
Boundary refinement (page 3) [v3.97]
12 evidence tracks + auto/manual		 14 evidence tracks weighted into a combined score. Half-strongest-edges → left/right boundary zones. ±5-window default zone. Hotkeys: E/F = manual override, B = save, A = accept, R = reset. Schema §12. R-side STEP_M06_emit_boundary_evidence.R emits all source layers post-v3.98.
Manuscript bundle export [v3.18]
Markdown + TSV + JSON		 Page 4 "📝 manuscript bundle" button. Emits a Markdown file (LLM prompt + candidate tables + per-candidate detail + BibTeX + example paragraph), a TSV (raw rows), and the JSON candidate list.
SHIPPED · earlier (v3.20 → v3.98)
Schema v2 layer awareness [v3.20]
_layers_present + registry export		 Scrubber detects schema version and tracks state.layersPresent as the source of truth for conditional UI. Schema badge in header. Page 4 has "📋 export registry (TSV+JSON)" and "+ load enrichment" drag-drop. SCHEMA_V2.md documents the full contract.
Page 7 ancestry view [v3.21]
drag-drop phase4_ancestry.json		 Per-window ancestry stack (max-Q label, delta12 confidence). Drag-drop loader for <chrom>_phase4_ancestry.json. View chips for label / Q-value / delta12. Reuses the popstats stack architecture.
Page 6 popstats (v3.40s) [v3.20s]
stacked tracks aligned to chrom		 θπ, Fst, dXY, MI, Hp, Tajima's D — one-track-per-row stack rendered to a single canvas. Drag-drop additional layers. Edge labels per track explain top/bottom range.
Page 4 karyotype + page 9 confirmed [v3.16/v3.17]
per-sample band table + confirm carousel		 Page 4 per-candidate sample table: CGA, ind, family, ancestry, σ-profile, locked band, K-cluster. Sortable + filterable. TSV export. Page 9 confirmed-candidates carousel — once a candidate is marked confirmed on page 2 it joins the carousel.
Catalogue page (page 4 / now page 4) [v3.15]
candidate list with persistence		 state.candidateList = saved candidates (vs. state.candidate = current focus). localStorage persistence per-chrom. Add / remove buttons on page 2. Candidate JSON import/export so list survives F5 / cross-machine.
Page 2 candidate focus [v3.14/v3.15]
deep-dive on locked candidate		 Lock colors on page 1 → ⬆ promote → page 2 shows: header (chrom, range), σ-profile chart, band composition cards, notes textarea, confirm button. Sample-σ distance chart (v3.40s) added on top.
Lasso selection mode (v3.39) [v3.39]
Shift+drag manual groups		 Shift+drag a box on the main PCA → captures samples into a new manual group (lasso_N). Color mode flips to 'manual'. Existing manual-group exclusivity rule applies (one sample per group). Per-sample lines: lasso-mode dim grey for tracked, accent gold for lassoed.
L3 K=6 / K=3+6 modes [v3.31]
sub-resolved haplotypes		 Toolbar K=3 / K=6 / K=3+6 buttons. New l2GroupCacheAtK Map keyed by ${l2idx}_${K}. compareL2Pair_atK at K=6 brute-forces 720 permutations for Hungarian alignment (<1 ms). Both stacked when K=3+6.
L3 contingency colors (shared/unique/dual) [v3.31]
pane palette modes		 shared = focal palette across all panes (default). unique/pane = per-pane palette + swatch legend. dual = fill from pane / ring from focal-aligned. Helps visualize K cluster identity continuity vs per-pane local structure.
Anchor concord strip (V bar) [v3.52]
tracked-samples K-means continuity		 Cramér's V between tracked-sample K-means labels at the anchor window vs all other windows. Hot = high concord, cold = low. Click-to-jump.
Sim minimap (sticky sidebar) [v3.51]
"move to minimap" toggle		 Sim heatmap can move into a small ~200px panel above the sidebar Data section, freeing main-grid vertical space. Click-to-jump on the minimap. Restore button returns it to the main panel.
Layout modes: fixed / free / compact [v3.62/v3.63]
3-state cycle button		 fixed = explicit grid-template-rows (everything fits on screen). free = block flow, panels stack at min-heights, scrollable. compact = 2-column upper region (PCA left, Z + tracks + lines right) + L3 spanning both at bottom. localStorage persistence.
Panel reorder (▲▼ buttons) [v3.59]
user-defined panel order		 State state.panelOrder persists user's preferred panel sequence (sim → Z → lines → anchor → PCA → L3 by default). Small ▲/▼ buttons in panel corners shift each panel up or down. Active in fixed mode (compact has its own CSS-defined order).
Sidebar collapse [v3.50s]
320px → 28px slide		 Sidebar collapses to a thin gutter (28 px) with a single ▶ expand arrow. data-sidebar="collapsed" on .wrap drives the grid-template-columns transition. Persists.
Light / dark theme toggle [v3.30s]
🌙 / ☀ button in header		 Full palette swap via CSS custom properties. themeColor() helper used by all canvas renderers so they pick up the active theme without manual color literals.
11-page tab architecture [v3.30s]
global ⚙ + 11 tabs (1–9, 11)		 1 diagnostic / 2 candidate focus / 3 boundaries / 4 catalogue / 5 karyotype / 6 popstats / 7 ancestry / 8 windows / 9 confirmed / 11 help. Tab buttons clone a single CSS pattern; .page.active shows the right container.
Relatedness JSON loader [v3.30s]
family palette + dropdown filter		 Drag-drop ngsRelate-derived JSON (with kinship θ, family memberships). Builds state.familyPalette for color mode='family'. Dropdown filter narrows samples to one family or hub.
Color modes: cluster / family / ancestry / manual [v3.20s]
getSampleColor + colorModeBar		 Toolbar bar selects how each PCA dot gets colored. cluster = K-means labels at current L2 (or locked). family = family palette. ancestry = ancestry palette. manual = user lasso groups.
Tracked samples + trails [v3.20s]
N tracked + trail back sliders		 Sidebar (or compact-mode panel) selects up to 30 samples to render with full color + trail. Trail back N controls how many windows back show as fading dots. PCA scatter labels each tracked sample with its CGA. PCA1 sign-align reduces flip jitter.
Per-sample lines panel [v3.20s]
PC1 / PC2 / GHSL stacked		 One row per active source (PC1 default). Untracked samples = grey cloud (offscreen pre-rendered). Tracked = full color. Locked candidate fish_calls render dashed-red break segments at L2s where the fish's K=3 vote differs from its consensus regime.
Robust |Z| panel + boundary peaks [v3.20s]
color modes bands/gradient/zone/highlight		 Per-window robust |Z| (or ±Z) rendered as a scatter on a chromosome x-axis. Color modes: bands (pre-set thresholds), gradient (blue→red), zone (bright in focal L2), highlight (bright above threshold). L1/L2 zone bars at top. Boundary peaks rendered as colored triangles below the bars (color = support_class).
Sim_mat heatmap with smoothing scales [v3.20s]
precomp / nn40 / nn80 / nn160 / nn320		 Five smoothing scales (one is precomp 200×200, four are NN bandwidths). Click-to-jump anywhere. PDF-style sim/Z split optional. Fit-square button auto-sizes panel for ~real-size view (cap 2400 px).
L3 contingency tables [v3.20s]
Hungarian-aligned mini-PCAs		 For each focal L2, render contingency table + mini-PCA panes for ±N L2 neighbors. Layout options: Focal only / +Left / +Right / +L/R / ±2 / Dual. b.continuity carousel/draft/chrom modes. Live-dosage option. compareL2Pair uses Hungarian alignment so cluster labels align across panes. ⬗ unpin focal sticks the comparison to one L2.
PLANNED — schema-locked, awaiting R-side or calibration
Multi-evidence regime framework [v4.0 doctrine, t14e]
K-means bands are GEOMETRIC, not biological

Doctrine (per Quentin, t14e): K-means bands are provisional geometric clusters, not final biological regimes. Final regime calls require multi-evidence convergence. K=6 frequently represents nested substructure inside three major regimes — do NOT default to interpreting K=6 as six biological regimes. The paper-level call should usually use major regimes; higher K values are diagnostic / substructure layers.

Workflow:

  1. K-means proposes local PCA bands (K=3 default; K=6 for substructure check).
  2. Dosage + GHSL + heterozygosity + ROH/F_ROH + family/relatedness each independently validate or reject the biological interpretation.
  3. Candidate-level regime calls are assigned only when multiple evidence layers agree.

Per-band layer scope (refinement t14e+): not every layer informs every band equally. The convergence rule is scope-aware — a layer marked low_info for a given band does not push toward STRONG nor toward SUSPECT. See SCHEMA_V2.md §14.2.5 for the full scope table.

  • GHSL — informs ALL bands (every band has a haplotype signature).
  • Heterozygosity — primary scope is the HET band; homozygous bands have ~0% het by definition, so any apparent het differentiation in HOMOs is dosage noise, not signal.
  • θπ (per-band π) — informative for HET (mixing two haplotype families) and as a cross-band asymmetry probe (reduced π in INV-fixed bands).
  • F_ROH — primary scope is HOMO bands (high F_ROH there can mask the band identity as IBD).
  • Family — confounder check for all bands; family LD can produce any band.

UI plan — a single mode-switcher above the L3 panes (and on the karyotype page), with one button per evidence layer:

  • K-means — current behavior. PCA-derived geometric bands. Always available.
  • Dosage — per-band marker dosage pattern (mean dose by group, dose distribution). Validates geometric bands as REF/HET/INV-like.
  • GHSL — per-band haplotype-similarity-by-locus. Validates whether bands diverge in haplotype space.
  • Heterozygosity — per-band dosage heterozygosity. The HET band should sit between the two homozygous bands.
  • ROH / F_ROH — inbreeding / IBD confounder check. A "band" that is just a high-F_ROH cluster is not a regime.
  • Family / relatedness — kinship confounder check. A "band" that is family-pure (high fam_purity) is family LD, not a regime.

Convergence rule (provisional, lock after 226-sample LG28 calibration):

  • Strong regime = K-means + dosage + GHSL all consistent AND ROH + family layers do NOT explain the band geometry.
  • Suspect regime = K-means alone, with ≥1 confounder layer flagged.
  • Rejected band = ROH or family fully explains the apparent K-means band → it is NOT a biological regime.

R-side prerequisites (NOT YET WRITTEN — parallel chat needed):

  • STEP_R30_emit_per_band_dosage.R — for each L2 × K-band, mean / median / quartile of marker dose; emit to per_band_dosage JSON layer.
  • STEP_R31_emit_per_band_ghsl.R — per-band GHSL summary (mean within / between divergence).
  • STEP_R32_emit_per_band_het.R — per-band heterozygosity (per-sample, then per-band aggregate).
  • STEP_R33_emit_per_band_froh.R — per-band mean / max F_ROH (uses MODULE_3 output).
  • STEP_R34_emit_per_band_family.R — per-band family composition + fam_purity.
  • STEP_R35_compute_convergence_call.R — applies the convergence rule, emits per-(L2, band) status (STRONG / SUSPECT / REJECTED) into the candidates registry under characterization.regime_calls (Q1 in schema §13).

Schema impact: extends SCHEMA_V2.md §13 (three-axis framework) Q1 "What is it" — the regime call answer is the multi-evidence convergence verdict, not the K-means assignment. K-means assignment becomes a component of the answer (alongside dosage / GHSL / het / ROH / family). Per-band registry keys ~6 × n_bands × n_candidates. Catalogue gains a regime_evidence column showing which layers supported / rejected.

Already in flight (turn 14e): Tooltip on the K=3 / K=6 / K=3+6 buttons explicitly warns that K-means bands are geometric. K=6 button shows ⚠ icon. Help → roadmap (this entry) documents the doctrine. The mode switcher itself, the per-evidence renderers, and the convergence verdict are v4.0 work.

Why this matters: avoids the systematic risk of over-calling regimes from PCA geometry alone — a known failure mode in hatchery cohorts where ROH (inbreeding) and family LD can both produce "clean" K-means bands that are NOT inversions. Treating K-means as provisional protects the manuscript from peer-review rejection on confounder grounds.
Three-axis evidence framework [schema 2.12]
tier × completion × characterization		 The big one. Three independent axes per candidate, not one tier number: Axis 1 — Confidence tier (Tier 1/2/3/4/SV-only from independence layers A=local PCA, B=SV callers, C=GHSL, D=Fisher genotype-breakpoint association); Axis 2 — Completion (~320–370 registry keys per candidate, % filled, per-question breakdown); Axis 3 — Characterization (7-question convergence engine: Q1 What is it · Q2 What's inside · Q3 Boundaries · Q4 How formed · Q5 How old · Q6 How common · Q7 Is it real, with five statuses ANSWERED / PARTIAL / MEASURED / CONTRADICTED / EMPTY). Counter-evidence is first-class (CONTRADICTED is the most valuable status — it surfaces internal inconsistency). R-side scripts STEP_R20–R23 in parallel chat. Scrubber displays only — does not compute. New catalogue columns + filters + a per-candidate dashboard panel. Final values + thresholds locked after 226-sample LG28 calibration. See SCHEMA_V2.md §13 for the full contract.
BAM evidence layer [schema 2.11]
coverage_dip + discordant pairs + split reads		 9 IGV-style tracks at 1 kb resolution per candidate scan region: coverage_dip, discordant_orient_FF/RR/RF, tlen_anomaly, split_read_density, clip_density, mapq_drop (the only down-weighter, polarity -1), pair_orient_inv_rate (composite). R-side STEP_M07_emit_bam_evidence.R uses mosdepth + samtools view flag filters. Boundary scoring weights NOT YET LOCKED — pending real-data calibration. SCHEMA_V2.md §12.
IGV.js embedded viewer [schema 2.11]
per-candidate BAM slice rendering		 Embed igv.js below the boundaries-page tracks. Three deployment options: local-file picker, local-server (LANTA serve.py + SSH port forward), or pre-extracted region BAM slices via planned STEP_M08_extract_region_bams.sh. Recommended default: pre-extracted slices (no infrastructure dependency). Schema-adjacent (not part of JSON contract).
Marker module phase 13 [schema 2.2]
PCR diagnostic panels per candidate		 3–10 markers per candidate K=3 regime, panel quality scoring, leave-one-fish-out validation, multiplex Tm checks. R-side STEP_M01/M02/M03 planned. Page 10 already exists in the scrubber but stays hidden until marker_panel_summary JSON is loaded. SCHEMA_V2.md §10.
Band-diagnostics source layers [schema 2.3]
θπ + ROH + FROH		 Three R-side emits parked as planned: STEP_T06_emit_theta_pi_panel.R, STEP_R12_emit_roh_intervals.R, STEP_R13_emit_sample_froh.R. Power the het-shape sub-module on page 2. SCHEMA_V2.md §11.
Dosage heatmap (page 8 windows) [schema 2.4]
v3.94 detection done, R emit pending		 Detection logic + canvas renderer + colorbars + 5 left tracks + 1 top track + stripe-quality scoring built in v3.94 turn 1. Toolbar (100/200/500 bin + stripe-quality button), region-binding (static + live with debounce) built in v3.94 turn 2. R-side dosage_chunks emit script not yet written. STEP29 ordering layers exist but no JSON shim emits them.
L3 view in catalogue
sub-L2 cluster groupings		 Toggle exists in toolbar but renders "coming soon" until row-builder is specified. Need: per-(L2 × sub-cluster) row schema, sub-cluster labels (HOMO_1_sub1, HET_sub_noise, etc. per schema 2.10), then column set + sort logic.
Per-sample line coloring modes [scaffold done, v4.0 renderers pending]
color by K-means / het / GHSL / θπ / dosage / F_ROH / family

Per-sample lines panel + L3 contingency panes + tracked-samples scatter share a UI-wide coloring mode. Eight modes:

  • kmeans (default) — current behavior; categorical K-means band identity
  • dosage — per-window dosage value, blue→red diverging ramp
  • ghsl — per-window GHSL divergence, viridis sequential
  • het — per-window het indicator (0 / 1 / NA), categorical
  • theta_pi — per-sample π contribution, plasma sequential
  • froh — per-sample F_ROH, ice→red ramp (IBD load)
  • family — per-sample family ID, categorical (one color per family)
  • confounder_alert — derived flag: red when F_ROH high OR family dominant

Status (turn 14e+ continue): UI scaffold IS LIVE. The mode picker (color: [kmeans ▾] dropdown) sits next to the PC1/PC2 checkboxes in the per-sample lines header bar. State slot state.linesColorMode persists to localStorage. Validator refreshLinesColorMode() falls back to kmeans when the chosen mode's source layer isn't loaded; runs after every JSON load. Greyed-out options have explanatory tooltips like "Needs per_sample_theta_pi JSON layer". The dispatch hooks _resolveSampleColorByMode(si, gi, mode) + _resolveSampleScopeColor(si, mode) are STUBS that return null sentinels — callers fall back to existing K-means coloring. Switching modes today triggers redraws but visible behavior doesn't change. v4.0 work: replace the stub function bodies with per-mode renderers; no UI refactor needed at that point.

Per-window vs per-sample dispatch handled inside _resolveSampleColorByMode(si, gi, mode): modes that vary by window (dosage, het, ghsl, θπ) get per-window colors along the trace; modes that are per-sample (kmeans, family, F_ROH, confounder_alert) get one color per trace.

Why this matters for §14: flipping the coloring mode is literally "show me what this evidence layer sees" for the focal candidate. The mode picker is the visual companion to the multi-evidence regime framework. Per §14.2.5 scope, modes annotate themselves with primary scope (e.g., "het primary scope: HET band") so users don't misread expected null contrast on out-of-scope bands as mode failure.

R-side prerequisites: STEP_R36_emit_per_sample_theta_pi.R (new); STEP_R13_emit_sample_froh.R (already gating §11). The per-sample dosage-het derivation reuses existing dosage_chunks with no new R script. per_sample_theta_pi layer detection added to detectSchemaAndLayers in turn 14e+ continue — when the R script eventually emits it, the picker option auto-enables. SCHEMA_V2.md §15.
Toolkit containerization [schema 2.14, post-v4.0]
turnkey for any species

Vision (per Quentin, t14e): the entire inversion-discovery toolkit packaged so another lab can run it on their own catfish, fish, animals, or plants without reimplementing infrastructure. Schema-neutral pin — no JSON contract impact.

Order of attack (sketch only):

  1. MODULE_2 + MODULE_3 first — variants/structure + ROH foundation. Single Apptainer image driven by YAML config.
  2. R emit scripts — pure R, easy to package as Docker. Lets users scrub their own data without running the full cluster pipeline.
  3. MODULE_4 (SV) — DELLY + Manta inherit from upstream containers; main work is conda env orchestration.
  4. MODULE_5A2 — deepest integration. Dense window registry + multi-mode MDS + snake families + GHSL + breakpoint validation. Most code we own directly.
  5. L1 / L2 detection — currently embedded in MODULE_5A2; expose as standalone steps for envelope-only users.
  6. Catalogue + manuscript bundle — already portable; just reproducible HTML rendering for the catalogue export.

Stack split: Apptainer (Singularity) for HPC, Docker for single-machine. Most academic clusters refuse rootful Docker but accept Apptainer. Container hashes + Dockerfiles will live in their own repos when built; this is a roadmap pin only. Scrubber itself is already portable (single HTML, no infra needed). SCHEMA_V2.md §16.
Atlas mode switching [schema 2.15, post-v4.0]
Inversion Atlas ⇄ Diversity Atlas

Vision (per Quentin, t14e+ continue): a single button at the top of the scrubber toggles between two views of the same JSON.

  • Inversion Atlas (current default) — every page focuses on chromosomal inversion candidates: L1/L2 envelopes, K-means karyotype, σ-profile verdicts, breakpoint zones, marker panels, regime evidence. This is what v3.99 ships today.
  • Diversity Atlas (planned) — every page focuses on genome-wide diversity: θπ, F_ROH, ROH atlas, ancestry-Q stratification, kinship/relatedness, family-LD patterns. The same JSON, different interpretation layer.

How it works: the JSON stays loaded in memory — not freed, not refetched, not reparsed. Only the rendering layer flips. Per-page cache (state._l3CacheFp, state.__linesCache) stays populated so switching back is instant. New UI-only state.atlas slot ∈ {inversion, diversity}, persisted to localStorage. Per Quentin's wording: "the JSON is unloaded — I mean loaded but in stand by."

Page mapping: most tabs have an analogue in both atlases (e.g. tab 3 is "boundaries" in inversion mode and "ROH atlas" in diversity mode; tab 9 is "confirmed candidates carousel" vs "saved regions carousel"; tab 11 is hidden in diversity mode). Catalogue forks: inversion catalogue (today) vs region catalogue. Manuscript bundles fork too. Tabs 5 (reports), 6 (help), 7 (ancestry) are atlas-shared.

What stays the same: schema, candidate registry (saved candidates keep IDs + confirmed flags; in Diversity Atlas they read as "regions of interest"), favorites, theme, layout, lasso selections.

Phasing: Phase A schema-only (now). Phase B toggle button + state slot. Phase C per-page Diversity-mode renderers (multiple chats). Phase D catalogue/bundle forking. Estimate ~3-5 weeks of post-v4.0 work for the full Diversity Atlas. SCHEMA_V2.md §17.
Thai language support (i18n) [schema 2.16, later]
Lab in Thailand · nice-to-have

Vision (per Quentin, t14e+ continue): the scrubber's strings (button labels, tooltips, panel headers, help-page content) available in Thai for the lab in Thailand. Tagged "later" — the lab can read English. Thai font is a known typographic challenge.

Architecture: state.lang ∈ {en, th}, persisted to localStorage. data-i18n="key" attribute pattern on HTML markup; t(key) lookup helper for dynamic JS strings. Translation table is a flat key/value map per locale, ~300-400 strings total at full coverage.

Font handling: extend the --font-base CSS variable's fallback chain to include "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai". Thai-specific CSS (line-height: 1.6, word-break: keep-all) applied to html[lang="th"]. Easy path: rely on system fonts (most modern Thai users have Noto installed). Bundled path: ~80 KB woff2 from Google Fonts CDN. Self-hosted path: vendor inline as base64 (offline-friendly).

Coverage tiers: T1 nav (~30 strings) · T2 nav tooltips (~30) · T3 page-2 candidate focus (~50) · T4 catalogue + windows (~80) · T5 help page (~150) · T6 status messages (~50). MVP = T1 + T2 + Thai font CSS = ~60 strings, manageable scope, big visible payoff.

What stays English regardless: code identifiers, JSON field names, scientific terminology (haplotype, PCA, F_ROH), citation strings, gene names, file paths, numeric formatting (Arabic numerals universal in scientific contexts). SCHEMA_V2.md §18.
14-axis classification (R-side) [schema 2.16, post-v4.0]
scrubber UI shipped · R emit pending

Status: scrubber-side UI scaffold IS shipped (Karyotype/Tier sub-view on page 4). What's PLANNED is the cluster-side R-pipeline that emits the final_classification JSON layer the Tier view consumes.

The 14 axes (recovered from past chat history of characterize_candidate.R + classify_inversions.R):

  1. existence_layer_a / b / c / d — pass/fail per layer (Local PCA, SV callers, GHSL, Fisher genotype-bp)
  2. boundary_quality — sharp / fuzzy
  3. group_validation — NONE / UNCERTAIN / SUPPORTED / VALIDATED / SUSPECT
  4. internal_structure — clean / gradient / composite_undecomposed
  5. recombinant_class — none / gene_conversion / double_crossover / mixed
  6. family_linkage — multi_family / few_family / single_family / pca_family_confounded
  7. polymorphism_class — cohort_wide / lineage_restricted / family_restricted
  8. mechanism_class — NAHR / NHEJ / MMBIR
  9. age_class — young / intermediate / ancient (Tajima D coalescent proxy)
  10. burden_class — enriched / neutral / depleted (deleterious-variant load)
  11. confidence_tier — T1 / T2 / T3 / T4 / SV_only / Layer_C_only (synthesis)

R-side TODO: migrate characterize_candidate.R and classify_inversions.R to phase 4e of the cluster pipeline, populate the registry per §13's three-axis framework, emit final_classification JSON layer + sibling TSV (final_catalog.tsv) keyed by candidate_id. Both scripts exist in past sessions but consume "old ad-hoc TSVs" — migration is mechanical once the v10 phase 4 library is in place.

Coexistence: final_classification.confidence_tier === §13 Axis 1 tier (must agree). §13 is the WORKFLOW (Q1-Q7 questions); §19 is the OUTPUT catalog. Read-only — scrubber never writes. SCHEMA_V2.md §19.
Local PCA scrubbers — page 1 (|z|) + page 2 (thetapi) [scaffold shipped, schema 2.19]
two-scrubber design · genotype + diversity · orthogonal validation

Two scrubbers, symmetric naming: page 1 ("local PCA |z|") runs local PCA on dosage. Page 2 NEW ("local PCA thetapi") runs the SAME pipeline on per-sample θπ instead. Same panel layout (sim_mat heatmap, |Z| waveform, per-sample lines, K-means scatter, L3 contingency). A region that hits BOTH scrubbers' candidate lists is ~certainly real biology — that's the orthogonal validation. A region that hits ONLY the θπ scrubber is something the genotype scrubber can't see (sweeps, balancing selection, Het-class subdivisions, mis-assemblies) — that's where new biology lives.

Where it lives: NEW user-visible tab position 2 ("local PCA thetapi"). DOM id is page12; existing pages 2-11 shift one slot right in the tab bar's user-visible numbering, but their DOM ids stay unchanged so all existing code references continue to work.

Panel layout mirrors page 1 (same vertical sequence, parameterized by which layer to read):

  • #thCtrlBar — scrubber slider (mirrors #ctrlBar)
  • #thSimPanel — θπ_sim_mat heatmap with L1/L2 overlays (mirrors #simPanel)
  • #thZPanel — diversity-|Z| waveform (mirrors #zPanel)
  • #thLinesPanel — per-sample θπ-PC1 lines (mirrors #linesPanel)
  • #thPcaPanel — θπ-PC1 vs θπ-PC2 K-means scatter (mirrors #pcaPanel)
  • #thL3Panel — diversity L3 contingency (mirrors #l3Panel)

Data sources — what already exists (per SCHEMA §22.13): theta_pi_per_window ALREADY EXISTS in production. STEP_Q05_aggregate_theta.sh in phase_5_qc_triage/ produces theta.<CHR>.<SCALE>.tsv.gz (per-sample per-window θπ from ANGSD pestPG). export_precomp_to_json.R --theta <Q05_output> already embeds this as w.theta (an array of n_samples values per window) inside each window object. The scrubber's reader auto-detects this Shape A even without an explicit _layers_present declaration. So if your JSON was exported with --theta, the page-12 scrubber's first-required-layer indicator already flips to 🟢.

What's still needed: theta_pi_local_pca (NEW, R-side STEP_R40 — per-window local PCA on θπ profiles, emitting z, lam1, lam2, ratio, pc1, pc2, sim_mat, mds_coords with same shapes as page 1's top-level dosage arrays); theta_pi_envelopes (NEW, R-side STEP_R41 — L1, L2, candidate_intervals from thresholding the diversity-|Z|). Both ~150 lines of R, structurally mirroring the existing dosage local-PCA scripts with the input matrix swapped.

What's NOT this page: STEP_Q07_popstats.sh + region_popstats.c (Engine F) produce per-window per-GROUP population stats (Hudson Fst, dXY, Tajima's D for Hom1/Het/Hom2 groups). That's pooled-per-group, NOT per-sample — useful for the per-candidate concordance figure (§22.7, deferred) but NOT what page 12's chromosome-wide scrubber needs.

Cross-method validation (deferred): when BOTH dosage candidate_intervals (page 1) AND theta_pi_envelopes.candidate_intervals (page 2) load, the catalogue gains a "method" column with each candidate's provenance: dosage_only, theta_pi_only, both, or both_overlap_partial.

Status: structural scaffold IS shipped (page 12 DOM, tab button "local PCA thetapi", empty-state placeholder with live ⚪/🟢 layer-status indicators, layer detection extended for the three layers including Shape A auto-detection). Panel renderers ship as follow-up turns when STEP_R40/R41 emit data — reuses page 1's draw functions parameterized by which layer to read. SCHEMA_V2.md §22.
internal_ancestry_composition panel [planned, schema §22.14]
L2/L3 zoom-trigger · per-candidate panel

What: a per-sample composition classifier that, given a parent interval (L2 envelope, L3 sub-envelope, or any candidate), classifies each sample's internal Q-window pattern as one of: homogeneous, dominant_plus_secondary, two_block_composite, continuous_gradient, multi_block_fragmented, diffuse_mixed. Plus per-sample fragmentation score, internal entropy, block analysis. Existing Python implementation: internal_ancestry_composition.py (formerly nested_composition.py) in the inversion-popgen-toolkit.

Quentin's t14e+ continue framing: "Maybe it can be for page 1 and page 2 like activate nested composition detection ?? When in L2 or L3 or ?" — yes. Two render paths planned: (a) page 1 + page 12 zoom-trigger panel that activates when state.viewMode === 'l2' or 'l3'; (b) page 3 candidate-focus persistent panel.

Data already loaded: the same data.ancestry_sample matrix that page 7 (ancestry view) reads — already shipped via export_precomp_to_json.R --ancestry_samples. No new data dependency. Algorithm port to JS is ~100 lines of pure functions translating classify_structure() + analyze_parent_composition().

Naming care: the script's docstring carefully separates "internal ancestry composition" (data-structure: Q windows nested inside parent intervals) from "nested inversion" (the stronger biological claim of inversion-inside-inversion). The scrubber respects this distinction — a two_block_composite verdict is a diagnostic finding, NOT a classification. The interpretation step (mapping composite verdict → "this looks like a nested inversion") is a downstream inference layer that lives in the §21 internal_dynamics block + §19 internal_structure axis, not in the panel.

Status: NOT shipped. Schema §22.14 documents the design + render placement + algorithm sketch. Algorithm port + panel renderer + zoom-trigger wiring all wait for a follow-up turn. When §21 internal_dynamics blocks are loaded via §20 registry interop, the panel will prefer the precomputed result over re-running the classifier in JS.
DESIGN OPEN — needs spec before building
Custom 3-second tooltip popover
slower delay than native title=		 User asked for 3-second hover delay; native browser title= attribute uses ~1s OS-controlled delay (not configurable). Building a custom popover means: hover state machine, timeout management, positioning logic, accessibility (ARIA), keyboard navigation. Useful but a full feature on its own.
Per-sample H per band
inversion vs not inversion		 Compute heterozygosity for each sample inside the candidate window range, grouped by their locked band. Needs per-sample H emitted as a track (e.g. --track_h_persample). Once available, scrubber renders one bar chart per band on page 2 below the band-composition cards.
π / θ curve per band
nucleotide diversity profile		 Per-band θ along the candidate span requires the engine to compute θ separately for each k-label group. Then scrubber renders overlaid lines on the candidate page. Could also do this on page 1 as multi-track overlay if tracks are flagged as "stratified by band".
Gene annotations
GFF overlay + breakpoint genes		 Exporter reads a GFF3 file passed via --annotation file.gff3, embeds gene coords + symbols as genes array in JSON. Scrubber adds (a) thin gene track on page 1, (b) "genes spanned by candidate" panel on page 2, (c) "breakpoint-disrupted features" list. Multi-turn: parsing in R, schema extension, three rendering surfaces.
Breakpoint nucleotide context
±50 bp facets, left + right		 Needs reference FASTA piped into the exporter (--reference ref.fa). JSON gets breakpoint_context: { left_seq, right_seq, flanking_bp }. Scrubber renders a sequence panel on page 2. Lighter than IGV but still real work.
Resize handles between panels
drag every interface boundary		 Currently sim, Z, lines, PCA, L3 panels each have one handle. Adding handles between every interface element (sidebar/main divider, compact-mode column resize, every panel pair) means: (a) consistent split-pane logic, (b) min/max enforcement, (c) per-handle persistence, (d) graceful redraw on every drag end. Most done; remaining pairs need audit.
BLOCKED ON DATA — awaiting 226-sample LG28 calibration
v4.0 release
production lock-in		 Gating items: real-data validation on the 226-sample LG28 cohort across all 28 chromosomes; final weights calibrated for the BAM evidence layer (schema 2.11) and the three-axis framework (schema 2.12); per-cohort threshold values for the 7-question convergence rules. v3.99 is feature-complete for v4.0 except for these calibration steps.
